preview_text:
The model favours Bosnia and Herzegovina at 47% to win, but the match remains competitive. Qatar's 23% win probability reflects genuine threat, while the 31% draw probability suggests the sides are closer in expectation than a straightforward two-way split would imply.

The outcome distribution points to a tight contest. Bosnia and Herzegovina are modest favourites, but the 31% draw likelihood means a stalemate is nearly as probable as a Bosnia win. Qatar's baseline chance sits well below its opponents', yet the spread across all three outcomes is broad enough that no single result dominates the forecast. The match carries genuine uncertainty.

A marked gap separates the model and market consensus. Sportsbooks price Bosnia and Herzegovina at 59%, some 12 percentage points higher than the model's 47%, and price Qatar substantially lower at 16% versus the model's 23%. The draw sits slightly lower in the market at 25%. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these divergent readings.
